register.note: New hero is full-width, thumbnail-legible (two diverging lines + scatter) with mono figcaptions; the mistyped point now ties to the line with a dotted leader. Off 4 only on the top-band composition.

The honest dashed trend vs the dragged flat blue fit, the 'SLOPE 2.1 -> 0.4' readout, and the dotted leader from the line to the mistyped row depict leverage — the cause now on-canvas, not just asserted.
The see-frame carries its own caption, axis labels, and an in-figure teal row-to-point binding; short of Distill's every-segment labelling because the blue line/axes stay barer.
Warm cream, soft lines, AA-tinted underlined terms; the prior page-opening dead air is resolved by the hero. Off 4 by residual in-panel voids (hero top band, see lower-left).
Conservatively from stills: a single explanatory load motion (line drags flat), an object-constant stepper, and a live break-it toggle (slope/estimate snap) — explanatory, not decorative.

Exemplar-grade: amber=truth, blue=prediction, crimson=error rationed to only the one bad row and its wrong estimate (no all-red field), teal=active, carried identically across canvas, controls, and AA -ink prose terms.

hero.note: Hero clears both DELIGHT floors; the mistyped row is drawn at its real position with a dotted leader to the line it drags, so the leverage cause lives in the picture.

assessment.note: The closing transfer (transfer-sensor) is a genuine open write-your-own prompt with a revealed model answer, not an MCQ.

verdict.summary: Clears register 3 across the board (colour 4); the hero resolves the opening dead air and depicts the leverage mechanism with the mistyped row tied to the line it drags.
